title: The Dangers of Black Market Botox | Unlicensed and Unregulated Botox Dangers
filename: The Dangers of Black Market Botox ｜ Unlicensed and Unregulated Botox Dangers.en.vtt
excerpt: Why does it matter if you get your botulinum toxin from the manufacturer or from a recognized pharmacy or from botox dave on instagram what if it's a parallel import or an unlicensed product or maybe just something that someone's made in a lab what are the risks involved that's w…

segments.text: why does it matter if you get your botulinum toxin from the manufacturer or from a recognized pharmacy or from botox dave on instagram what if it's a parallel import or an unlicensed product or maybe just something that someone's made in a lab what are the risks involved that's what i'll be discussing today on the aesthetics mastery show [Music] it got me thinking when i first started in aesthetics there was a rather infamous case that really changed my mind about how important it is to use the right product and it was actually an american doctor who managed to get some research-grade botulinum toxin for a bargain price i'm sure and used it on himself and his girlfriend and his first two clients and all four of them ended up in itu on death's door with a massive overdose why does this happen of course botox is almost impossible to see the amount that you need to get an effective treatment

is impossible to see in the vial so if you end up with a vial with 10 times or a hundred times more botany toxin than you want you cannot tell by looking at it it's only then that you find out how strong your botulinum toxin actually is later on i went to imcas and someone had analyzed different fake products which they'd tested in a laboratory to see how effective they were and once again i was hit by the same surprising fact which is it wasn't that fake botox is weaker in fact many of the concoctions were actually stronger one of them was twice as strong as it was supposed to be according to the vial and this is much worse than if you were treated with a product that was weaker because if it's weaker then at least you might need a top-up or it may not work and you won't have the same treatment but the risk with a stronger dose is huge in fact it's unlimited the risk is death if you are over treated with a huge amount of botulinum toxin that's where this all started botulism is a lethal disease if you inject enough botox you will get all the symptoms of botulism of course now thankfully in the normal cosmetic doses

we are nowhere near enough to cause botulism type symptoms but if you are treated with a vial which may have several hundred times more of the actual active ingredient than it says on the vial you will get all of those symptoms you're effectively causing the illness and this is what happened to that american doctor who was testing his research grade product on his friends and family so how is this relevant to the other options you have in terms of buying products are you going to buy from botox dave who's got something that looks very similar or a different brand name but it comes from a country where it's used routinely what are the risks involved there now here i think it's important to understand that you're never just buying a vial of product you're buying the system that supports that product and this is very hard to understand because most of us just think well once it's in front of you it's likely to be the same it looks the same it'll probably do the same but the thing with particularly botulinum toxins is that they are part of one of the most sophisticated manufacturing and distribution processes that there are for any drug it's

extremely hard to manufacture extremely hard to make accurately as well one of the facts which i learned recently was that the entire world supply of botulinum toxin will fit into a teaspoon so think about how difficult it would be to distribute that evenly amongst all the millions and millions of vials that we use to make sure there is a consistent amount in each vial in many ways you're not actually paying for the protein you're paying for all the skill and the technology that it takes to accurately distribute just the right amount of botulinum toxin in these individual vials so that we get predictable results that is what the value comes from it's that consistency the validation is where we need to really appreciate what we've got in the products that we buy and that's the bit that you're missing if you start to use cheaper products from botox dave or from imports you're now buying a product that has no validation you have no idea how it was transported you have no idea whether this was a dodgy batch they wanted to get rid of or whether this is a batch

that was maybe falsely made in a different laboratory and labelled up differently and it's that lack of validation which is a major risk for using botulinum toxins that haven't come from legitimate sources so in the uk the authentic system is that you buy either directly from the manufacturer with what you're laughing at it's really hard to keep talking about stuff when somebody so in the uk the authentic botulinum toxins come from either regulated pharmacies or directly from the manufacturer everything else including online so-called pharmacies but often don't require a script but they're parallel importing you're buying a product which is much less validated you simply have a lot less certainty about what it is what the dose is and how much you're going to be injecting and in particular you know how well it's been cared for on that journey so you are taking on more risk than you would be with a legitimate process so we have four different grades we have the authentic system which i recommend that you use you then have your parallel imports of licensed products

you then have your licensed products which are parallel imported which means you've got less certainty about the system that supports them and then you've got your pure counterfeit products which are hard to spot but are the worst possible scenario to the lay person it's not necessarily clear about what the risk is especially if you you've occasionally bought a fake handbag in thailand you might just think well it's going to do something similar and of course it's really different it's better that you think of this maybe like someone offering you a flight abroad and he's not he's got his own airport he's built his own plane he's really good at diy and he's telling you it's going to be fine like that's your account of it then you've got the guy who's bought a secondhand boeing from russia and he's tell he promises you sorted it out nicely what do you think is a decent analogy or should i use something different to me the difficult point to make is

that validation is the most important part of what you're buying with your vial of botulinum toxin if you happen to buy a vial of the internet that works once that doesn't mean that it's going to work the next time and that it won't overdose someone another time so we're really purchasing certainty around what we use with our patients and your patients need to know that if you're legitimately spending more to keep your patients safe make sure that you mention it occasionally in your literature i wouldn't go on about it too often because there is that whole hygiene effect which is you shouldn't talk about things which people should assume are happening anyway but when you are asked have a good solid answer for it you're using legitimate products and don't be buying them off the internet and risking your patience and yourself
